notes: Twenty-first validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school provides a premier college preparatory education built on Core Knowledge, STEM emphases, citizenship, values, and classical education.
Official materials say one of its signature programs is the Classical Foundations Program, which brings academic and business leaders to speak with students.
The school says honesty is the bedrock value in its core values framework.
Its branding includes the school pledge line, "I Dare to Soar."
Official administration pages say Dr. Shelly L. Shope serves as CEO and Angela Brooks serves as headmaster.
Official board pages list Debbie A. Clary as board chair.
Its mission says the school aims to create a culturally responsive public education system that supports academic success and unlocks every child's unique gifts.
Official materials say the school was designed to connect at-risk Durham area youth to a wide array of intellectual and social capital resources, both high touch and virtual.
The school says its curriculum fosters curiosity and a sense of personal, financial, and academic responsibility.
Its vision describes a dynamic 21st-century franchise model of public education that builds bridges to the economic mainstream for vulnerable scholars.
Official board pages list Rev. Kenneth Hammond as chairman of the board.
Official board pages describe Rev. Kenneth Hammond as a co-founder of the school.
Its mission says the school offers a traditional curriculum while instilling character values and leadership development within a positive, challenging learning environment.
The school says its vision is to SOAR by serving the community and world as outstanding leaders who achieve through respect and responsibility.
Official materials say students can pursue three academic tracks: college preparatory, career preparatory, and occupational course of study.
A founding-board page says the school's founding board dates to 2010.
Official staff pages list Glenn Byrum as executive director and Jeremy Thompson as elementary school principal.

Official board pages list Erica Blackson, Katie Blixt, Kelly Dempsey, Dawne Dowdy, Rodward Hewlin, Mike Ladd, Eric LaForce, Kim Leppert, Stephanie Sherer, Garrett Tryon, and Kevin Wiley on the 2025-26 board of directors.
Its mission says the school prepares students to achieve scholastic, personal, and professional goals through a learner-centered educational program.
Official materials say the school opened in the 2025-26 school year.
The school brands its model around entrepreneurial learning and the Habits of Success.
Its vision highlights a rigorous academic foundation, an integrated curriculum focused on real challenges, and a commitment to underserved students.
Official board pages list John Oxaal as board chair and Trip Cogburn as founding head of school.
The school says charter schools are public schools of choice that are tuition-free, independently run, and funded primarily by state and local tax dollars.
Its homepage says, "We imagine an Inclusive Community where everyone Thrives."
A back-to-school message in August 2023 says the school was beginning its 10th year of educating students in western North Carolina.
Official newsletters say the school's work is grounded in six core values: Inclusivity, Academic Excellence, Strong Relationships, Well-being, Exploration, and Creativity.
Its homepage spotlight says the school hosted its first Social Emotional Learning Showcase.
Official pages say the school is a proud member of the Diverse Charter School Coalition.
Official pages list Jenn Townley as head of school.
